content: WYCKOFF ACCUMULATION — Phases A-E: SC extreme vol → AR sets range high. Phase B: SM accumulates in range. Phase C: THE SPRING — false drop below support LOW volume. HIGHEST PROBABILITY entry. Phase D: SOS break above AR high expanding vol. Phase E: Markup. KEY: Spring must be LOW vol — high vol = real breakdown.

WYCKOFF DISTRIBUTION — Mirror at tops. BC extreme vol → AR sets range low. Phase B: SM distributes. Phase C: THE UTAD — spike above resistance LOW volume, traps late buyers. Phase D: SOW break below AR low. Phase E: Markdown. UTAD must be LOW vol.

SPRING ENTRY — 1) Price below support. 2) Volume LOW. 3) Rapid recovery above support. 4) SOS expanding vol. Entry: After recovery + confirmation candle. Stop: Below Spring low. Target: Range high. Spring = institutional stop-hunt to fill orders.
EFFORT vs RESULT — Volume = effort. Spread = result. Agree = genuine trend. Diverge = absorbing. High vol + narrow spread = absorption. Most powerful VPA signal.

HIGH VOL + NARROW SPREAD = ABSORPTION — SM buying while capping price. At tops: distributing without letting fall. Tighter spread on high vol = larger institutional order. The #1 signal for detecting smart money activity.

BUYING CLIMAX — 1) Massive vol spike (highest in weeks). 2) Wide spread up. 3) Often gaps. 4) Followed by narrow spread or reversal. SM DISTRIBUTING. Crypto: highest hourly vol in 30+ days at cycle high + extreme positive funding.

SELLING CLIMAX — 1) Extreme vol spike. 2) Wide spread down. 3) Panic/capitulation. 4) Followed by narrow spread or reversal. SM ACCUMULATING. Crypto: highest daily vol in 90+ days + massive liquidation cascade.

TESTING — After high-vol event, price returns on MUCH LOWER vol. Low vol test of climax = exhausted (reversal). Low vol test of breakout = valid (continuation). HIGH vol on test = FAILURE. Most important volume confirmation.

BREAKOUT VOLUME — Genuine: 2-3x average expanding. Fake: low/declining. After breakout: elevated on continuation, contracts on pullbacks. Vol surge on pullback = FAILING. Compare to 20-period avg on spot volume.
VOLUME PRECEDES PRICE — Volume trends change BEFORE price. Declining vol in uptrend = waning buying. Declining vol in downtrend = sellers drying up. Rising vol before breakout = accumulation ending.

VSA THREE DIMENSIONS — 1) Volume (effort). 2) Spread (result). 3) Close position. Close HIGH + high vol = demand. Close LOW + high vol = supply. Close MIDDLE + high vol = ABSORPTION. Evaluate all three.
VOLUME AT S/R — Declining vol approaching support = HOLDS. Expanding vol approaching = BREAKS. Same for resistance. Highest vol level = most significant S/R.

HEAD AND SHOULDERS — Most reliable reversal. Left shoulder high vol, head LOWER, right shoulder LOWEST. Neckline break HIGH vol. Volume DECREASES left-to-right = KEY. Target: head-to-neckline projected. Right shoulder exceeding head = FAILS.
FLAGS & PENNANTS — Highest probability continuation. Declining vol, SURGE on breakout. Target: flagpole length. High & Tight Flags ranked #1 overall (69% avg rise, 1% failure).

CONFIRMATION RULE — Always wait for confirmation. Bullish: close above pattern high. Bearish: close below pattern low. Without = potential only. #1 rule for candlestick trading. Patience prevents losses.

LIQUIDATION CASCADES — 1) Price hits liquidation level. 2) Forced selling. 3) More liquidations. 4) Liquidity vacuum. 5) MM stabilizes. Entry: Buy V-recovery when OI dropped 20%+. Crypto's Wyckoff Springs.
VOLUME PROFILE S/R — HVN = acceptance, consolidates. LVN = rejection, moves quickly. Pullbacks TO HVN. Breakouts THROUGH LVN. POC = fair value. Value Area H/L = boundaries.
SWING STRUCTURE — Uptrend = HH + HL in swing points. Downtrend = LH + LL. Trend REQUIRES 2+ swings. Foundation of all analysis.
CONTEXT OVER SIGNAL — Perfect signal in WRONG context = loser. Context: market state, S/R location, MTF alignment, volume, session. Assess CONTEXT first, then signals.
H1/H2 ENTRIES — H1: First pullback in bull trend, buy above its high. H2: Second pullback (two-legged), more reliable. Often at trendline/MA. L1/L2 for downtrend. Bread-and-butter trend entries.
TWO-LEGGED PULLBACK — Highest probability entry. Leg 1, bounce, leg 2. Entry after 2nd leg exhaustion (narrow bars, lower vol). Stop below pullback low. Most corrections have two legs.
FAILED SIGNAL / TRAP — Entry bar reverses, trapping traders. Trade trap in opposite direction. Failed moves = FAST opposite moves. Trapped traders exiting = momentum. Most breakouts fail.
PERCENT RISK SIZING — Equity × Risk% ÷ (Entry-Stop) = position. Standard 0.5-1%. Auto-adjusts: bigger winning, smaller losing. Anti-martingale. Position sizing THE most important factor.
2% RULE — Never risk >2% per trade. $100K = $2,000 max. Calculate before entry. Professionals risk LESS. If stop would risk >2%, reduce size or skip.
DRAWDOWN RULES — 3 losers: stop day. 5: stop week. 10% DD: reduce 50%. 20%: minimum size. 25%: close all, break. Capital preservation > profit.
ROLE REVERSAL — Broken support = resistance. Broken resistance = support. All timeframes. Break with volume. Retest = entry with stop beyond. Most reliable when tested multiple times before breaking.
UTAD SHORT — Spike above resistance LOW vol, reverses. Entry: Short after falls below resistance + vol confirm. Stop: Above UTAD high. Target: Range low. HIGH vol breakout = genuine, do NOT short.

CRYPTO SPRING — BTC: 1) Drop below key support. 2) Liquidation cascades. 3) Vol spikes then dries up. 4) Recovery within 1-3 candles. 5) OI decreases. Entry: Buy recovery. Stop: Below spring 1-2 ATR. Expect 5-15% below support.

CRYPTO DISTRIBUTION — BTC tops: 1) Parabolic on declining vol. 2) BC on highest vol months. 3) Drop then weak rally. 4) Lower highs. 5) UTAD (low vol). 6) Break below. Whale wallets to exchanges + extreme funding = distribution.

COMPOSITE OPERATOR CRYPTO — ETF custodians, MicroStrategy, whales accumulate/distribute over weeks-months. 1) Accumulate during fear. 2) Distribute during euphoria. 3) Create ranges. 4) Use springs/UTADs. Whale wallet balance reveals.
SIGN OF STRENGTH — After Spring: 1) Breaks AR high. 2) Expanding vol. 3) Pullbacks low vol. 4) Higher highs. Entry: First pullback after SOS. Stop: Below last higher low. Validates Spring was trap.
STACKED IMBALANCES — Buy:sell ratio >3:1 across 3+ price levels. Become reliable S/R. Aggressive buyers defend zone. Use as pullback entry zones.
OI + PRICE — Rising OI + rising price = healthy. Rising OI + falling = new shorts. Falling OI + rising = covering (weak). Falling + falling = liquidation (near bottom). New trend requires RISING OI.
OI DIVERGENCE — Rising price + falling OI = weakening (only covering). Falling price + falling OI = weakening (only liquidation). New ATH with declining OI = spot-driven without leverage conviction.
HEALTHY TREND VOL — Uptrend: vol EXPANDS rallies, CONTRACTS pullbacks. Downtrend: opposite. Unhealthy = contracts on rallies or expands on pullbacks = distribution/accumulation.
VOLUME DIVERGENCE — Bearish: price HH but vol LOWER. Bullish: price LL but vol LOWER. Confirm by price action. Divergence = WARNING not signal. 3+ consecutive = stronger.
CLIMAX VOLUME — 3x+ 20-period avg at extreme, then reversal/narrow range. MAJOR turning point. Crypto: highest vol bar in 50+ periods at cycle extreme = climactic reversal.
NO SUPPLY — Bearish candle, VERY LOW vol, NARROW spread in downtrend. Sellers EXHAUSTED. Bullish at support after decline. Entry: Buy confirmation above bar high. Stop: Below bar low.
NO DEMAND — Bullish candle, VERY LOW vol, NARROW spread in uptrend. Buyers EXHAUSTED. Bearish at resistance after advance. Entry: Short confirmation below bar low. Stop: Above bar high.
UPTHRUST — Wide spread UP high vol, CLOSES near LOW. Spike up then slammed. TRAP for buyers. Short confirmation below upthrust low. Stop: Above high.
SHAKEOUT/SPRING — Wide spread DOWN piercing below support then reversing. Low vol = spring (trap). High vol = real breakdown. Must RECOVER above support quickly. Buy recovery.
VOL DRY-UP BEFORE BREAKOUT — Most powerful breakouts preceded by lowest vol in 20+ periods. A/D complete. Breakout on SURGE. Declining exchange vol + BB squeeze + flat OI in crypto.
RISING MKT + FALLING VOL — HH on progressively lower vol = distribution. SM selling each rally. Volume trend reversed. Do NOT add to longs. Tighten stops.
FALLING MKT + FALLING VOL — LL on progressively lower vol = accumulation. Sellers drying up. SM buying. Watch for bottom. Crypto: declining exchange reserves + declining spot vol at lows.
H&S TOPS — Avg decline 29% bear, 34% bull. Failure 1% bear. Throwback 64%. Vol increases on breakdown. Complex (multiple shoulders) similar. Ranked #6 bear.
INVERSE H&S — Avg rise 30% bear, 41% bull. Failure 4%. Pullback 51%. Vol increases on breakout. Buy above neckline. Most reliable bottom reversal.
DOUBLE BOTTOM — A&A (two narrow V): 37% bull, strongest. E&E (two rounded): 36%, ranked #6. Confirmed on break above confirmation high. Vol lower on second test = KEY.
DOUBLE TOP — E&E (two rounded): 25% bear decline, failure 2%, very reliable. Second peak vol LOWER. Confirmed on break below confirmation low.
TRIANGLE BREAKOUT — Symmetrical: neutral. Ascending: bullish. Descending: bearish. Vol CONTRACTS. Breakout 1/2-3/4 of triangle. Vol 2x+ avg. Target: widest part projected.
THROWBACK/PULLBACK — 62% bull breakouts throwback. 54% bear pullback. Avg retrace 48%, 10-15 days. Resumes 67% of time. Buy throwback at breakout level. HIGH vol throwback = failure.
PATTERN FAILURE — Pattern failing its measuring implication signals OPPOSITE direction. H&S failing neckline = strong bullish. Triangle breakout reversing = trade opposite. Failure is information.

BULLISH/BEARISH ENGULFING — White engulfs black after downtrend (bullish). Black engulfs white (bearish). Three criteria: trend, wraps, opposite colors. Stronger if first small, second large. Heavy vol increases significance.
HAMMER — Small body at top, long lower shadow (2-3x body) after downtrend. Requires bullish confirmation (close above hammer high). Strong at support. Crypto: wick >3x body = reversal.
MORNING/EVENING STAR — Three-candle reversal. Star = indecision. More powerful if doji. Vol increases on third candle. Key reversal at S/R.

CANDLE S/R — Bearish engulfing/dark cloud highs = resistance. Bullish engulfing/piercing lows = support. Cluster at same level = very strong zone. Long upper shadows = resistance. Long lower = support.
GAP/WINDOW — Rising window: continuation, gap = support. Falling window: gap = resistance. Three windows = exhausted but valid until last closed. Fill on high vol = failure. CME BTC gaps fill ~90%.
REVERSAL BAR — Bull: close above open/midpoint, lower tail 1/3-1/2 bar. Bear: mirror. Followed by strong entry bar. Strongest at S/R. Entry above/below. Stop beyond extreme.
TWO-BAR REVERSAL — Bull: bear + bull closing above both opens. Bear: mirror. Entry on break of 2nd bar. Stop beyond pattern. Most common reversal all timeframes.
INSIDE BAR — Range within prior. Contraction. Breakout setup either direction. ii = tighter. iii = tightest, highest prob. Stop orders both sides.
MEASURED MOVE — Leg 2 = Leg 1 distance. All timeframes. Exact = reversal likely. Exceeding = acceleration. Short = deceleration. Most common target method.
S/R FLIP — Broken support = resistance. Broken resistance = support. Retest = 'last kiss' entry. Stop beyond level. More touches before break = stronger flip.
BREAKOUT PULLBACK — After strong breakout bar, wait 1-3 bars pullback (<50% of breakout). Enter on first trend-direction bar. Stop beyond breakout bar. High probability.
CLIMAX REVERSAL — Extended trend + 5+ trend bars, huge bar + immediate reversal. Don't chase. Wait for 2-legged correction. Often followed by range, not immediate reversal.
BREAKOUT TEST — After breakout, pullback to test level. Holds = continuation. Breaks back = reversal. Buy/sell test. Stop beyond. Higher probability than chasing.
FAILED H2/L2 — When H2/L2 fails, becomes strong OPPOSITE signal. Failed bear setup = strong bull. Trade failure with tight stop beyond failed signal.
IMPULSE vs CORRECTIVE — Impulse: <30% overlap, closes near extremes, vol expands. Corrective: >60% overlap, closes middle, vol contracts. 30-60% = uncertain.
FAILED BREAKOUT SETUP — Breakout reverses immediately. Trade OPPOSITE. Entry on re-entry to range. Stop beyond failed extreme. Target: opposite side. Often larger than original.
MTF STRUCTURE — Higher TF: context. Trade TF: setups. Lower TF: precision. Disagreement = reduce size or skip. Higher ALWAYS overrides lower.
TIGHT RANGE BREAKOUT — 5-20 bars, narrow range. Breakout closes beyond on volume. Enter above/below. Stop inside range. Failed = trade opposite. Powerful reversal.
CANDLE BODY READING — Large body = conviction. Small = indecision. Close near high = bullish, near low = bearish. Growing = strengthening. Shrinking = weakening.
DRILL-DOWN — HTF for bias, LTF for entry. 60min + 5min. Only when aligned. Checklist: 1) HTF direction. 2) Pullback to S/R. 3) Rejection candle. 4) Enter on break. 5) Stop beyond zone.
PULLBACK ENTRY — Wait for trend, enter on pullback. 1-3 candles, <50% of impulse. First reversal candle in trend direction. Stop beyond extreme. BE at 1:1.
FALSE BREAK — Price breaks then reverses. Enter counter-breakout. Stop beyond false extreme. Target: opposite side. More convincing = stronger reversal. More profitable than real breakouts.
CONFLUENCE ZONES — Prior S/R + round number + MA + trendline + Fibonacci at same level. More = stronger. Single-factor weaker. 3+ = highest probability.
TRENDLINES — Uptrend along LOWS. Downtrend along HIGHS. 2 points min, 3 confirms. More touches = valid. Longer = more significant break. Heavy vol break = reversal.
TREND CHANNELS — Parallel to trendline. Failing to reach = weakening. Exceeding = accelerating. Break = potential change. Sell at upper in uptrend. Redraw as accelerates.
GAP TYPES — 1) Common: fills, avoid. 2) Breakaway: high vol, new trend. 3) Runaway: mid-trend. 4) Exhaustion: extreme vol, end. 5) Island: gap+gap = reversal. Low vol gap = meaningless.
ALLIGATOR — Jaw (13 SMMA offset 8). Teeth (8 offset 5). Lips (5 offset 3). TWISTED = sleeping (no trades). DIVERGE = eating (trend). Lips cross above Teeth+Jaw = BUY. Below = SELL.
ATR-BASED STOPS — 1.5-2x ATR(14). Adapts to volatility. High vol = wider, smaller position. Low vol = tighter, larger. Place beyond technical barriers. NEVER widen. If >2%, skip.
SCALE-OUT — Full entry. First target: sell half, stop to BE. Second: half remainder, stop to entry. Trail last quarter. Guaranteed profit + runners.
6% RULE — Monthly equity drops 6% below last month's close → STOP ALL TRADING. Close positions. Prevents many small losses destroying account.
IMPULSE SYSTEM — GREEN (MA up + MACD-Hist up) = buy only. RED = sell only. BLUE = caution. Never long on red, short on green. Buy pullbacks to green bars.
TRIPLE SCREEN — Weekly: trend direction. Daily: oscillator pullback. Intraday: precision breakout. Factor of Five (~5x). Higher ALWAYS overrides lower.
SQUEEZE PLAY — BB inside KC = squeeze (energy building). BB expands outside = FIRES. Use momentum wave for direction. Only dominant wave direction. Tighter = bigger move.
CONFLUENCE IS KING — Single signals fail. Highest probability: 1) S/R zone. 2) Trend. 3) Candle pattern. 4) Indicator. 5) Session. 3+ = take. <3 = skip.
PA HIERARCHY — 1) Structure (trend/range). 2) Levels (S/R). 3) Patterns (signals). Always this order. Never skip to patterns without context.
SECOND ENTRY — Second attempt higher probability. First resistance test fails = second more likely to break. Failed H2 = strong bull. Failed L2 = strong bear.

FUNDING RATE — Positive = longs paying (crowded). Negative = shorts. Extreme positive (>0.1%) = correction probability (squeeze). Extreme negative = short squeeze. Funding + OI divergence = strongest.
DIVERGENCE — Price HH + RSI/MACD LH = bearish warning. Price LL + oscillator HL = bullish. Strongest at S/R. WARNING not signal. Wait for price confirmation.

UNEXPLAINED BREAKOUTS — Nobody understands reason = good risk/reward. LESS explanation = MORE significant. Everyone observing = higher false signal odds. Unexplained from tight ranges = institutional.
FAILED MOVES = FAST OPPOSITE — Market not responding to bullish news = strong bearish. Not responding to bearish = strong bullish. Failure is the signal. Trade opposite aggressively.
AUTOMATIC RALLY — After SC, first rally on moderate vol sets range high. AR = resistance during Phase B. SC low = support. Trade within SC-AR until Phase C/D.

VSA CORE — No Supply: low vol + narrow spread + close near high at support = buy. No Demand: mirror at resistance = sell. Stopping Volume: high vol at support, narrow spread. Effort vs Result: high vol narrow spread = reversal.
BID-ASK SPREAD — Narrowing = liquidity, trending. Widening = uncertainty. Trade breakouts when narrow. Fade when wide. Crypto: thin book = rapid moves through zone.
VP + VSA — VP for S/R zones (HVN/LVN). VSA within zones for SM intent. VP = LOCATION. VSA = INTENT. Together = WHERE and WHEN.
HIGH VOL + WIDE SPREAD UP — Genuine buying. SM driving + retail following. At breakouts and impulse legs. Do NOT fade. Continuation confirmed.
LOW VOL + WIDE SPREAD = TRAP — Looks impressive but nobody participated. Low vol breakout = fake. Widening spread on declining vol = exhaustion.
LOW VOL + NARROW SPREAD — No interest, QUIET BEFORE STORM. After prolonged = explosive move. Direction by which stops trigger first.
UTAD — Spike above resistance after prolonged distribution to trap late buyers, reverses. FINAL TRAP before markdown. Short on reversal candle closing back inside range.
HEALTHY TREND — Vol expands on rallies, contracts on pullbacks. Opposite = unhealthy. Any deviation = weakening.
CLIMAX VOLUME — 3x+ 20-period avg at extreme + reversal/narrow range. MAJOR turning point. Highest vol in 50+ bars at extreme.
VPA SIZING — Larger when vol confirms (high vol breakout). Smaller when contradicts. Base 1% + 0.5% per confirmation factor.
SPRINGBOARD — Declining vol + narrowing range = SM completed A/D. Breakout EXPLOSIVE. Tighter + lower vol = bigger move. Enter aggressively on breakout.
VOL COMPARISON — Compare to: 1) Prior bar. 2) 20-period avg. 3) Same level historically. 4) Similar conditions. Only meaningful RELATIVELY.
GAP VOLUME — High vol gap = institutional, holds as S/R. Low vol = retail, fills. Fill on high vol = failure. Hold on pullback = continuation.
TWO-BAR REVERSAL VOL — Wide spread bar + equal/wider opposite on equal/higher vol at extreme = powerful reversal. Second bar MUST close beyond midpoint of first.
HIDDEN BUYING — Price drops then recovers to close near HIGH on above-average vol. Selling ABSORBED intrabar. High vol hammer at support = high prob long.
HIDDEN SELLING — Price rallies then reverses to close near LOW on above-average vol. Buying ABSORBED intrabar. High vol shooting star at resistance = high prob short.
GAPS AND VOLUME — Breakaway: high vol at pattern break. Runaway: mid-trend moderate. Exhaustion: extreme vol at end. Island: gap+gap = reversal. Low vol gap = meaningless.
VOL IN TRIANGLES — CONTRACTS during formation. Breakout MUST expand. Low vol breakout = false, returns inside. Vol at apex = lowest.
VOL IN FLAGS — Flagpole highest vol. Consolidation declining. Breakout renewed expansion. Vol increase during flag = distribution, fails.
VOLUME TRENDLINES — Draw on volume like price. Vol trendline break PRECEDES price break. Rising vol break in uptrend = selling pressure.
CUP AND HANDLE — U-shaped + shallow handle. Cup depth 1/3-1/2. Handle declining vol, max 1/3 cup retrace. Breakout SURGE. Target: cup depth. 47% avg rise bull.
WEDGES — Rising = bearish. Falling = bullish. Vol DRYS UP. Unlike triangles, both lines same direction. Target: widest part.
RECTANGLES — Horizontal S/R. Vol contracts. Breakout on volume. Target: height from breakout. Often continuation. Wait for close beyond boundary.
PERFORMANCE RULES — 1) Lowest failure = best. 2) Heavy breakout vol. 3) Throwbacks hurt 6-8%. 4) Tall > short. 5) Wider swings = stronger. 6) Narrow breakouts fail.
DEAD CAT BOUNCE — Drop 20%+, bounce 15-20% in 4-8 days, resume decline. Bounce vol low. Sell into bounce. Decline continues 30%+.
ISLAND REVERSAL — Gap in trend direction, isolated, gap BACK. Vol heavy on both gaps. Powerful reversal at major turns.
MEASURED MOVE — Two legs separated by correction. Second = first ~60%. Use first leg for target. Retracement 49% (down), 27% (up).
BROADENING — Expanding range (megaphone). Often at TOPS. Vol heavy, erratic. Each swing wider. Wait for resolution. Breakout = significant trend start.
TRIPLE BOTTOMS/TOPS — Three equal lows/highs. Vol decreases on each test. Buy above resistance / Short below support. 37% avg rise (bottoms), 24% decline (tops).
PIPE/HORN PATTERNS — Two parallel spikes. Pipe bottom ranked #2 overall bull (39% rise). Pipe top #3 bear (27% decline). Buy above / Short below.
DOJI — Open = close. After extended move = reversal warning. Dragonfly = bullish. Gravestone = bearish. At S/R after trend = actionable.
DARK CLOUD / PIERCING — Dark cloud: opens above prior high, closes >50% into body. Piercing: mirror. More penetration = stronger. Bearish engulfing is stronger version.
THREE SOLDIERS/CROWS — Three consecutive long candles same direction. Opens within prior body. Strong continuation. Rare but powerful.
HARAMI — Large + small within prior body. Momentum shift. Harami cross (doji second) = stronger. Always requires confirmation.
MARUBOZU — Long body, no shadows. White: total bull control. Black: total bear control. Strong continuation or reversal by context. Don't fade.
SHOOTING STAR / HANGING MAN — Small body, long upper shadow after uptrend. Rejection of higher prices. Hanging man needs bearish confirmation next day.
TREND BAR — Body >60% of range. Close near extreme. One side in control. Stronger beyond prior extremes. Series = strong trend, don't fade.
MICRO-CHANNEL — Every bar low above prior (5-10 bars). EXTREME strength. Don't short. Don't buy breakout (climax). Wait for correction.
MAGNET LEVELS — Price gravitates to: prior swing H/L, round numbers, open, prior day H/L. Use for targets. Opening price = intraday magnet.
SPIKE AND CHANNEL — Strong breakout + shallow channel. Buy pullback to channel bottom. Spike retrace 50%+ = ending. Channel break against = trend change.
BARB WIRE — 3+ bars with significant overlap. Choppy. AVOID. Wait for breakout. Often after strong move. Place stops beyond range.
TREND FROM OPEN — Strong directional from open, minimal pullbacks. Enter on first small pullback. Don't fight. First 2-3 trend bars = confirmed.
GAP OPEN — Gap with trend = continuation. Fade against. Fill ~70% common. Breakaway ~30%. Doesn't fill in 3 bars = strong direction.
BREAKOUT SETUP — Tight range, breakout closes beyond on expanding vol. Entry above/below. Stop opposite side. Failed = trade opposite.
FIRST PULLBACK — After breakout/impulse, wait for first pullback. Entry at S/R within pullback or when closes in trend direction. Highest probability.
TREND EXHAUSTION — 1) Impulses shrinking. 2) Corrections deepening. 3) Vol declining on impulse. 4) Failed channel line. 5) Divergences. 3+ = reduce.
TRADE QUALITY — A = all aligned (full size). B = most (normal). C = some conflicting (reduced). D = skip. Prevents overtrading.
RANGE STRATEGY — Buy support, sell resistance with limits. Stop beyond boundary. Target opposite. Reduce size. Low vol at boundary = holds. High vol = breakout.
TREND STRENGTH — Strong: large impulses, <38.2% retrace. Moderate: 38.2-61.8%. Weak: >61.8%, shrinking. Strong = aggressive, weak = defensive.
OVERLAP REGIME — <30% = impulse (trending). 30-60% = uncertain. >60% = corrective/ranging. Objective measurement for strategy selection.
REACTION CANDLE AT S/R — Strong bullish candle at support, body closes upper half. Entry above candle. Stop below zone. Mirror at resistance. HTF alignment increases probability.
SESSION ANALYSIS — London open: direction. NY open: confirm/reverse. Overlap: highest vol. Afternoon: ranging. Crypto: US drives BTC. London = breakout catalyst.
CANDLE SEQUENCE — 3+ consecutive same color = that direction. Alternating = ranging. Growing bodies = strengthening. Shrinking = weakening.
FAN LINES — Three successively less steep from same point. First = watch. Second = higher probability. Third = REVERSAL CONFIRMED.
ROUND NUMBER S/R — Psychological levels attract vol. High vol + rejection = strong. High vol + acceptance = A/D. Breakout requires exceptional vol.
FRACTAL — UP: bar high > 2 bars each side. DOWN: mirror. Broken = continuation. BUY on break above. SELL below. Fractal + AO positive = strongest.
AO ZERO CROSS — Neg to pos = buy. Pos to neg = sell. Never trade against. Wait for candle close. Increasing = accelerating.
DIVERGENCE BAR — Bull: lower low, close in upper quarter. Bear: higher high, close in lower quarter. Entry above/below bar. Strongest at S/R.
MFI STATES — GREEN (MFI+ Vol+): continuation. FADE (MFI- Vol-): pausing. FAKE (MFI+ Vol-): false, reverses. SQUAT (MFI- Vol+): breakout imminent.
CONVERGENCE — 1) Alligator eating. 2) AO correct. 3) Divergence bar. 4) MFI green. 5) Fractal breakout. 3+ = elevated prob. 5 = max size.
S/R ZONES — Areas of repeated reversal. Line charts to find. Old > new (memory). Market scars. Width = timeframe. More touches = stronger.
LAST KISS — After break through zone, wait for retest. Enter on retest. Stop beyond. Confirms breakout. 60-70% occurrence. Better than chasing.
BIG SHADOW — Range engulfs prior at zone. Bullish at support = buy. Bearish at resistance = sell. Enter on break. Stop at opposite end.
KANGAROO TAIL — Long-wick at zone, small body. Longer wick = stronger. Enter on body break. Stop at tail tip. Trendy (with-trend) > counter-trend.
ZONE MTF — Weekly/daily for zones. 4H/1H for entries. Line chart for zones, candlestick for entries. Multi-TF confluence increases probability.
WAVE 3 — 1.618x wave 1. BIGGEST money. AO strongest. Entry after wave 2 (50-61.8% retrace), AO turning. Best risk/reward. Cannot be shortest impulse.
WAVE 5 TRUNCATION — Fails to exceed wave 3. AO divergence + lower high. Powerful reversal. Short with stop above wave 3.
GAP FILL — Common ~70%. Breakaway ~30%. Trade fill then reassess. 3 bars no fill = strong direction. CME BTC gaps ~90% fill.
PIVOT POINTS — Pivot = (H+L+C)/3. R1 = 2P-L. S1 = 2P-H. Test daily pivot 90%. R2/S2 = dead H/L. Pivot + round number + MA = strongest confluence.
OPENING GAP — Low premarket vol = 85% fill. High vol = 60%. Tue-Fri only, within pivots. Entry at open, target prior close. Monday 55-60%.
TREND ID — 200 SMA: above = uptrend, below = downtrend. 50 SMA intermediate. Golden cross = major bullish. Death cross = major bearish.
EMA PULLBACK — 20 EMA = dynamic support in uptrend. Pullback + bounce. Entry on bullish candle at EMA. Stop below swing. ADX>25 for confirmation.
PRICE IS PRIMARY — All indicators lag price. Price action at zones = faster info. Use indicators for confirmation only. Market speaks through bars.
SESSION-BASED PA — Asian: quiet. London: trend initiation. NY overlap: highest vol. Afternoon: ranging. Crypto: US drives BTC. London = breakout.
TREND RESUMPTION — After pause: continuation = tight range, declining vol. Reversal = wide range, heavy vol. Trade breakout direction.
REVERSAL DAY — Opens near extreme, closes opposite after trend. Daily/weekly significant. High vol = MAJOR turn. Low vol = minor pause.
CHANNEL BREAK — Break with trend = acceleration (hold). Break against = trend ending (exit). Narrow = strong trend. Wide = weak.
INSIDE DAY BREAKOUT — 2+ inside days. Buy above / sell below range. Stop-and-reverse opposite. Compression before expansion.
ADX FILTER — >25 = trending (trend tools). <20 = ranging (oscillators). 20-25 = transition, reduce size. Rising = strengthening. Falling = weakening.
TIME STOPS — No profit in expected timeframe = exit. Loss after 1-2 weeks = wrong. Even breakeven after time = probably wrong. Complements price stops.
PROCESS OVER OUTCOME — Execute plan not P&L. Good process + bad outcome = correct trade. Track execution quality separately from profitability.
NO SETUP = NO TRADE — Only when all criteria met. Quality > quantity. Patience is edge. No clear setup = best trade is no trade.
FAILED MOVES — Market 'should' do something but doesn't = trade opposite. Not responding to news = opposite direction signal. Failure is information.
TREND FOLLOWING — Go with trend. Whatever method, get in when major trend exists. Consistency > particular characteristics. 15-30% of time = profits.
CONTRARIAN AT EXTREMES — Best at new highs (sell). Worst at new lows (buy). Best money at turns. Need technical confirmation.
DO NOTHING — Most people always playing. Wait until money lying in corner, pick it up. Trade as little as possible.
EXCHANGE FLOW — Inflows = selling pressure. Outflows = accumulation. Sustained outflows during decline = SM accumulating. Inflows during rally = distribution.
VOL IN ELLIOTT — Wave 3 highest vol. Wave 5 lower (divergence). Wave A high. Wave B lower. Wave C high. Volume confirms wave count.
ROUNDING BOTTOMS/TOPS — Gradual U/inverted U. Vol mirrors shape. Months to complete. Major trend changes. Buy on breakout above rim.
MEASURED MOVE DETAIL — Second leg equals first ~60%. Use first leg height for target. Classic swing pattern on all timeframes.
RISING/FALLING THREE — Long candle, three small within range, then another long. Continuation. Fifth confirms. Three small = brief pause.
ABANDONED BABY — Rare: star gaps away, next gaps back. Very rare and reliable. Strongest at S/R extremes.
WAVE 4 — Ends 38.2% of wave 3. Most reliable wave 5 entry. Cannot overlap wave 1. AO pulls to zero. Enter when AO turns back.
CHANNEL DETAIL — Width = volatility. Narrow channels = strong trend. Wide = weak. Exceeding = climax. Break = potential trend change.
ORDER BOOK — Large walls can be pulled (spoofing). REAL = persisting >5 minutes. Thin = rapid movement. Thick = stalls. Wall removal before price = trap.
LIQUIDATION HEATMAP — Price gravitates to liquidation clusters. Long pool below = drop magnet. Short pool above = rise magnet. Trade toward clusters.
FRIDAY CLOSE — Strong Friday close at yearly highs significant. Don't hold short with loss at Friday high. Weekend gap favors trend.
TIME-BASED WRONGNESS — Loss after 1-2 weeks = clearly wrong. Breakeven after time = probably wrong. Don't hold dead positions.
DIVERGENCE DETAIL — 3+ consecutive divergences = stronger warning. Must wait for price confirmation. Divergence alone never sufficient.
VOLMAN ON INDICATORS — All lag price. Derive from price. Why use derivative when you can read source? Confirm only, never lead.
OUTSIDE BAR — Range engulfs prior. Expansion/volatility. Closes near extreme = continuation. Closes near middle = neutral. oo pattern = very volatile.
BELT-HOLD — White: long white opens at low in downtrend. Black: long black opens at high in uptrend. Strong single-bar reversal. More reliable on daily+.
SCALLOPS — Ascending/descending J-shaped patterns. Each scallop trends higher/lower. Ascending inverted scallop: 43% avg rise bull.
THREE FALLING PEAKS / RISING VALLEYS — Each peak lower (bearish) or each valley higher (bullish). Short below valley / Buy above peak between.
TWO-BAR VOL — Bar1 high vol up + Bar2 low vol narrow = demand exhausted. Bar1 high vol down + Bar2 low vol narrow = supply exhausted. Both high same dir = continuation.
ABC CORRECTION — A-wave against trend, B-wave partial retrace, C-wave continuing. Entry after C-wave at structural level. Most common corrective pattern.
BIAS RESET — Reset at session start. Don't carry yesterday's bias. First 30-60 min establishes new. HH/HL = bullish. LH/LL = bearish. Neutral until proven.
PARTIAL PROFITS — Take 50% at 1:1 R:R. Trail remainder. Reduces emotional pressure. Ensures winner even on reversal. Standard for active traders.
RANGE TRADING — If price bounced off same S/R 3+ times, it's a range. Buy support, sell resistance. Stop beyond range. Target opposite end. ADX <25.
COUNTERATTACK LINES — Strong trend, opposite candle opens with gap but closes at prior close. Bullish: gaps down recovers. Bearish: gaps up fades. Moderate reversal.
TWEEZERS — Two+ candles with equal highs (top) or lows (bottom). Minor reversal. More significant with other patterns. Equal H/L = strong level.
STALL PATTERN — Price moves to level then pauses with 2-3 small bodies. Equilibrium at level. Breakout direction from stall continues. Enter on breakout bar.
TTM WAVES — A (short-term), B (medium), C (long-term) around zero. BUY: C above zero + squeeze fires long + A dips then crosses up. Never fight C wave.
TRADE REVIEW — Review every trade for setup quality, entry, management. A/B/C categorization. Only A setups when starting. Track win rate by quality.
TREND THEORY — Primary: months-years. Secondary: weeks-months (1/3-2/3 retrace). Minor: days-weeks. Bull = HH+HL. Reversal = LH+LL. Foundation of Western TA.
SCHABACKER FOUNDATION — Chart patterns repeat due to human psychology. All subsequent pattern work builds on this. Patterns = psychology made visible.
VOLUME AS TRUTH — Price can be manipulated short-term. Volume reveals truth. Every source emphasizes volume confirmation. Volume = one indicator that cannot be faked.
FRACTAL THEORY — Markets fundamentally fractal. Same patterns at every timeframe. 5-min looks like daily looks like weekly. Use MTF analysis. High fractal dim = choppy. Low = trending.
DOW THEORY — 1) Discount everything. 2) Three trends. 3) Three phases. 4) Indices confirm. 5) Volume confirms. 6) Until definitive reversal. Patterns = specific tools.

PATTERN RELIABILITY — Larger > smaller. Longer formation > shorter. At major S/R > random locations. Edwards/Magee rank: 1) H&S. 2) Rectangles. 3) Symmetrical triangles. 4) Double/triple. 5) Flags/pennants. 6) Wedges. 7) Broadening.

WHY PATTERNS WORK — Human nature is repetitive. Greed and fear create recognizable footprints. Institutional behavior forced by size creates predictable patterns. Self-fulfilling prophecy (everyone watching same levels).
PATTERN TYPES — Reversal: H&S, double/triple tops/bottoms, rounding. Continuation: triangles, rectangles, flags, pennants, wedges. Too long continuation = becoming reversal.
SM DETECTION FRAMEWORK — Coulling VPA (volume-price divergence) + COT (hedger positioning) + Wyckoff (A/D) = three independent methods for institutional activity detection.
PA PRINCIPLE — Price is the ultimate indicator. Everything needed is in the bars. No lagging indicators needed. Market speaks through price. Learn by studying bar-by-bar action.
